questions.prompt: Minimum viable account structure for a single-product DTC brand?

choices.text: Three campaigns: 1 ASC prospecting (CBO, 8-15 ads, existing-customer cap set), 1 retargeting campaign (1-2 ad sets covering cart-abandoners and site visitors, 3-5 ads), 1 brand/seasonal campaign (often paused outside launches). Consolidation beats fragmentation: each ad set hits learning threshold, audience overlap is contained, signal is readable, hygiene is light.

questions.explanation: 1 ASC + 1 retargeting + 1 brand/seasonal. Three campaigns covers most accounts.

questions.prompt: Daily ops only for 8 months, no monthly/quarterly review, performance slowly worse. Fix?

choices.text: Add monthly audit (2-3 hours) and quarterly lift testing. Skipped higher-order rhythms cause silent decay: stale CRM uploads, lookalike seed age, audience overlap drift, AEM event priority issues, retired campaigns lingering — each adds 2-5% efficiency loss that compounds to 20-30% over months. Quarterly lift grounds attribution; otherwise scaling decisions ride on inflated numbers. The fix is rhythm, not more daily intervention.

questions.explanation: Add monthly + quarterly rhythms. Daily isn't the leak; higher orders are.

choices.text: Tracking → structure → targeting → creative → bidding → macro. Tracking first because pixel/CAPI bugs masquerade as performance issues — broken measurement and real demand collapse look identical in the dashboard. After verifying tracking, check learning-phase status and audience overlap before changing creative. Most issues live in layers 1-4; only after exhausting them should you blame seasonality or macro shifts.

questions.explanation: Tracking → structure → targeting → creative → bidding → macro.

questions.prompt: Ad set stuck in 'Learning' for 6 weeks. Cause and fix?

choices.text: Under 50 optimization events per week. Causes: budget too low for the CPA (e.g., $20/day with $20 CPA = 7 events/week), optimization event too rare (Complete Registration getting 20/week), audience too small, or bid cap too tight. Fixes: consolidate budget into fewer ad sets so each crosses the threshold, switch optimization to a higher-volume upstream event (InitiateCheckout instead of Purchase), open targeting, or remove caps. Ad sets that never exit learning never have stable EAR predictions.

questions.explanation: Under 50 events/week. Consolidate or shift event.

questions.prompt: When is narrow targeting + manual + cost cap the right pattern?

choices.text: B2B with small structural audience (e.g., 200k decision-makers at specific company types), regulated verticals requiring explicit audience definitions, high-AOV niche with weak pixel signal, or hyper-local services. The pool is structurally too small for broad to be efficient; ASC's broad-by-design fights the offer's natural audience; cost cap enforces predictable CPA against a tight margin. The three deviations stack because the underlying account isn't median.

questions.explanation: Small structural audience + tight margin = narrow + manual + cost cap.
